trans-unit.source: 🚧 This section of the training platform and the entire content is under ⚠️construction⚠️ and may not be shared or published! 🚧
trans-unit.target: 🚧 This section of the training platform and the entire content is under ⚠️construction⚠️ and may not be shared or published! 🚧

trans-unit.source: Task 2 - Access healthcare - avoid areas
trans-unit.target: Task 2 - Access healthcare - avoid areas

trans-unit.source: For the main area of Kutupalong Refugee Camp, we have the boundary, the path and road network, water streams and health facilities. In this task we will compare openrouteservice isochrone and QGIS built-in service area catchments. Also we will showcase the avoid area feature in openrouteservice to account for a simple simulated flooding.
trans-unit.target: For the main area of Kutupalong Refugee Camp, we have the boundary, the path and road network, water streams and health facilities. In this task we will compare openrouteservice isochrone and QGIS built-in service area catchments. Also we will showcase the avoid area feature in openrouteservice to account for a simple simulated flooding.

trans-unit.source: Download all datasets __[here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Modul_9/Modul_9_Exercise_2_Access_healthcare_avoid_areas/Modul_9_Exercise_2.zip)__. Save the folder on your computer. Unzip the .zip file. The unzipped folder is structured according to the recommended folder structure for QGIS projects. Under "data > input" you find the geopackage file `task2.gpkg` which contains the following vector layers:
trans-unit.target: Download all datasets __[here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Modul_9/Modul_9_Exercise_2_Access_healthcare_avoid_areas/Modul_9_Exercise_2.zip)__. Save the folder on your computer. Unzip the .zip file. The unzipped folder is structured according to the recommended folder structure for QGIS projects. Under "data > input" you find the geopackage file `task2.gpkg` which contains the following vector layers:

trans-unit.source: `camp18_healthcare` (points): Healthcare facility locations within camp 18 (from `OpenStreetMap`)
trans-unit.target: `camp18_healthcare` (points): Healthcare facility locations within camp 18 (from `OpenStreetMap`)

trans-unit.source: `camp18_ways` (points): Roads and paths within camp 18 (from `OpenStreetMap`)
trans-unit.target: `camp18_ways` (points): Roads and paths within camp 18 (from `OpenStreetMap`)

trans-unit.source: `camp18_streams` (points): Water streams within camp 18 (from `OpenStreetMap`)
trans-unit.target: `camp18_streams` (points): Water streams within camp 18 (from `OpenStreetMap`)

trans-unit.source: `camp18_boundary` (polygons): The camps legal boundary (from `HDX`)
trans-unit.target: `camp18_boundary` (polygons): The camps legal boundary (from `HDX`)

trans-unit.source: STEP 1: Healthcare catchment - Isochrones
trans-unit.target: STEP 1: Healthcare catchment - Isochrones

trans-unit.source: First we need to reproject our layers. To do so, open the `Processing Toolbox` and search for `Reproject layer`. Select one of your layers as your input layer and choose **WGS 84 / UTM zone 46N** as your target CRS. Maybe you have to click on the planet button on the right and search for it, if it is not in the list. Leave all other settings at default. Repeat the process with all your layers to reproject all of them.
trans-unit.target: First we need to reproject our layers. To do so, open the `Processing Toolbox` and search for `Reproject layer`. Select one of your layers as your input layer and choose **WGS 84 / UTM zone 46N** as your target CRS. Maybe you have to click on the planet button on the right and search for it, if it is not in the list. Leave all other settings at default. Repeat the process with all your layers to reproject all of them.

trans-unit.source: To get the isochrones click in the toolbar on the ORS Tools plugin Icon. Click on `Batch Jobs` -> `Isochrones from Layer`. Leave all settings at default except: | | | |------------------------------|------------------------------| | Travel mode | foot-walking | | Input Point layer | camp_healthcare | | Comma separated ranges | 5, 10 |
trans-unit.target: To get the isochrones click in the toolbar on the ORS Tools plugin Icon. Click on `Batch Jobs` -> `Isochrones from Layer`. Leave all settings at default except: | | | |------------------------------|------------------------------| | Travel mode | foot-walking | | Input Point layer | camp_healthcare | | Comma separated ranges | 5, 10 |

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Watch here:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Watch here:
:::

trans-unit.source: STEP 2: Healthcare catchment - QGIS Service area
trans-unit.target: STEP 2: Healthcare catchment - QGIS Service area

trans-unit.source: Isochrones in QGIS can be generated via the service area tool in combination with the minimum bounding geometry tool. Based on a road network, we define origins, a default speed, and a maximum cost.
trans-unit.target: Isochrones in QGIS can be generated via the service area tool in combination with the minimum bounding geometry tool. Based on a road network, we define origins, a default speed, and a maximum cost.

trans-unit.source: Open the `Processing Toolbox` and scroll down to `Network Analysis`, choose `Service area (from layer)` or enter "Service area (from layer)" in the search bar. Leave all settings at default except: | | | |--------------------------------------|-------------------------------------| | Vector layer representing network | camp_ways | | Path type to calculate | shortest | | Vector layer with start points | camp_healthcare | | Travel cost | 500 (meters) | | **Advanced Parameters** | | | Default speed | 5.0 (pedestrian) | | Topology tolerance | 50.0 |
trans-unit.target: Open the `Processing Toolbox` and scroll down to `Network Analysis`, choose `Service area (from layer)` or enter "Service area (from layer)" in the search bar. Leave all settings at default except: | | | |--------------------------------------|-------------------------------------| | Vector layer representing network | camp_ways | | Path type to calculate | shortest | | Vector layer with start points | camp_healthcare | | Travel cost | 500 (meters) | | **Advanced Parameters** | | | Default speed | 5.0 (pedestrian) | | Topology tolerance | 50.0 |

trans-unit.source: QGIS on-the-fly creates a network graph from the road and path network. The graph is rather simple. All edges can be traveled in both ways, there are no weights included. Travel cost is represented by the length of a segment and the default average speed. The output is a simplification of the input road & path geometry but with the attributes of the healthcare facilities. For every healthcare facility we see a multiline geometry object in the output. We can style it according to the attribute “@osmId” to see which segments are assigned to which facility.
trans-unit.target: QGIS on-the-fly creates a network graph from the road and path network. The graph is rather simple. All edges can be traveled in both ways, there are no weights included. Travel cost is represented by the length of a segment and the default average speed. The output is a simplification of the input road & path geometry but with the attributes of the healthcare facilities. For every healthcare facility we see a multiline geometry object in the output. We can style it according to the attribute “@osmId” to see which segments are assigned to which facility.

trans-unit.source: Your result should look like this:
trans-unit.target: Your result should look like this:

trans-unit.source: STEP 3: Service area by minimum bounding box
trans-unit.target: STEP 3: Service area by minimum bounding box

trans-unit.source: In order to better compare the result of the service areas and isochrones we will again compute the minimum bounding geometry. But this time for the multiline output of the service area tool.
trans-unit.target: In order to better compare the result of the service areas and isochrones we will again compute the minimum bounding geometry. But this time for the multiline output of the service area tool.

trans-unit.source: Open the `Processing Toolbox`, choose `Network Analysis`,then choose `Minimum Bounding Geometry`or enter "Minimum Bounding Geometry" in the search bar. Leave all settings at default except: | | | |------------------------------|------------------------------| | Input layer | Service area (lines) | | Field | @osmId | | Geometry type | Convex Hull |
trans-unit.target: Open the `Processing Toolbox`, choose `Network Analysis`,then choose `Minimum Bounding Geometry`or enter "Minimum Bounding Geometry" in the search bar. Leave all settings at default except: | | | |------------------------------|------------------------------| | Input layer | Service area (lines) | | Field | @osmId | | Geometry type | Convex Hull |

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Watch here:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Watch here:
:::

trans-unit.source: STEP 4: Healthcare catchment - Isochrones avoid flood
trans-unit.target: STEP 4: Healthcare catchment - Isochrones avoid flood

trans-unit.source: In this part we will again calculate catchments based on the same configured isochrones. But we will include a polygon for the avoid area functionality in openrouteservice. For the **avoid area** we will use the water streams that run through the main camp area. The avoid areas function of openrouteservice only allows polygons as input, not line geometries. Therefore we need to conduct some preprocessing steps:
trans-unit.target: In this part we will again calculate catchments based on the same configured isochrones. But we will include a polygon for the avoid area functionality in openrouteservice. For the **avoid area** we will use the water streams that run through the main camp area. The avoid areas function of openrouteservice only allows polygons as input, not line geometries. Therefore we need to conduct some preprocessing steps:

trans-unit.source: Reproject the the camp_stream layer to be able to use a metric distance to buffer. Like before use **WGS 84 / UTM zone 46N** as the target projection.
trans-unit.target: Reproject the the camp_stream layer to be able to use a metric distance to buffer. Like before use **WGS 84 / UTM zone 46N** as the target projection.

trans-unit.source: Now buffer the reprojected stream layer into a polygon layer with a distance of 2 meters
trans-unit.target: Now buffer the reprojected stream layer into a polygon layer with a distance of 2 meters

trans-unit.source: We are set to use the buffered stream layer as avoid area or exclusion layer for the routing.
trans-unit.target: We are set to use the buffered stream layer as avoid area or exclusion layer for the routing.

trans-unit.source: Icon
trans-unit.target: Icon

trans-unit.source: Click in the toolbar on the ORS Tools plugin Icon [#$sd1_sf15_dp1]. Click on `Batch Jobs` -> `Isochrones from Layer`. Leave all settings at default except: | | | |------------------------------|------------------------------| | Travel mode | foot-walking | | Input Point layer | camp_healthcare | | Comma separated ranges | 5, 10 | | **Advanced Parameters** | | | Polygons to avoid | stream_buffer |
trans-unit.target: Click in the toolbar on the ORS Tools plugin Icon [#$sd1_sf15_dp1]. Click on `Batch Jobs` -> `Isochrones from Layer`. Leave all settings at default except: | | | |------------------------------|------------------------------| | Travel mode | foot-walking | | Input Point layer | camp_healthcare | | Comma separated ranges | 5, 10 | | **Advanced Parameters** | | | Polygons to avoid | stream_buffer |
